name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 10 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, formTrigger, wait, splitOut. It contains 25 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note9

parameters.content:
=======================================
            WORKFLOW ASSISTANCE
=======================================

Scrape Glassdoor Job Listings For Prospecting with Bright Data and LLMS

For any questions or support, please contact:
    Yaron@nofluff.online

Explore more tips and tutorials here:
   - YouTube: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
   - LinkedIn: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
=======================================
Bright Data Docs: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}


*Important*
Make Sure To Add Your API Keys to the HTTTP REQUESTS NODES (BRIGHT DATA API), GOOGLE RELATED NODES AND LLM NODE

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
🔍 Glassdoor Jobs Scraper – Parameter Guide
Use this object to query Glassdoor jobs.
Each field filters results appropriately.


{
  "location": "{{ $json.Location }}",
  "keyword": "{{ $json.Keyword }}",
  "country": "{{ $json.Country }}"
}
🧾 Field Explanations & Valid Options
🗺️ location
City or region for the jobs.
✅ Example: "Berlin", "New York"

🧠 keyword
Job title or keyword to match.
✅ Example: "Data Scientist", "Marketing Manager"

🌍 country
ISO two‑letter country code.
Use two letters like US, FR.

✅ Full Example
{
  "location": "Berlin",
  "keyword": "Data Scientist",
  "country": "DE"
}

This HTTP Request sends a POST call to Bright Data to start a new dataset snapshot based on your filters.

👋 If you don’t want to use the Form Trigger,
you can directly adjust the filters here in this node.

nodes.name: Basic LLM Chain

parameters.text:
=Read these fields from the job post:
- Company: `{{ $json.company_name }}`
- Title: `{{ $json.job_title }}`
- Description: `{{ $('Google Sheets - Adding All Job Posts').item.json.job_overview }}`

**Task**  
1. If this role relates to marketing, content creation, or audience engagement, write **1–2 concise icebreaker sentences** that:
   - Reference the company or job context  
   - Explain how our Content Repurposing service can help
Make sure to add the compnay name and job title.

Note that we're not pitching based on the job title.
Were pitching to the organization only if the job position they are looking for, can be fulfilled by our agency.

Example:
Hey,
I've noticed your'e looking for {{ $('Google Sheets - Adding All Job Posts').item.json.job_title }}.

I have an offer that might be relevant to your team.

Then transition to our offer of content repurpose
2. Otherwise, reply with:  
---JOB POST NOT RELEVANT---

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On form submission - Discover Jobs".
Open the "Sticky Note9" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Snapshot Progress" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note10" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP Request- Post API call to Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait - Polling Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If - Checking status of Snapshot - if data is ready or not" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP Request - Getting data from Bright Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets - Adding All Job Posts" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Basic LLM Chain" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets - Update Pitches" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
